theme: Nova webhook delivery failures silently dropped for 6 hours plus Atlas retainer signing and Vertex SSO security vulnerability disclosed

events.text: Customer Holdar Corp reported missing webhook events — their integration silent since 12:04, 6 hours of missed deliveries
NOVA-1001 Critical — Holdar Corp webhook delivery silently failing since 12:04, 6-hour gap, 3,847 events not delivered
Engineering: checking webhook delivery logs for Holdar Corp since 12:04
test: pull Holdar Corp webhook delivery log since 12:04 — identify failure mode
Infra: Holdar Corp webhook endpoint returning 200 but event queue shows 3,847 pending — delivery worker not processing their queue
NOVA-1002 opened — Holdar Corp webhook delivery worker stuck, queue growing since 12:04
Engineering: delivery worker deadlock — Holdar Corp's worker thread acquired queue lock at 12:04 and never released
REVERT: webhook-delivery-worker.py — lock acquisition without timeout introduced in 11:50 deploy
Revert deployed — deadlocked worker thread killed and restarted, queue processing resuming
NOVA-1002 resolved — deadlock cleared, delivery worker restarted, queue processing resuming
Queue drain starting — 3,847 events being delivered at 800 events per minute, ETA 4.8 minutes
NOVA-1003 opened — 3,847 event queue drain in progress at 800 events per minute
PM calling Holdar Corp integration lead Yusuf — explaining 6-hour gap and current drain status
Subject: Webhook delivery gap — Holdar Corp | Body: 3,847 events queued and delivering now, expected complete by 18:08, written root cause to follow
Queue drain: 800 of 3,847 delivered — 20% complete
Security disclosure received — researcher Ananya Iyer reporting stored XSS in SSO tenant name field, CVE being assigned
VTX-701 Security Critical — stored XSS in SSO tenant name, researcher requesting 7-day disclosure window
Security lead: stored XSS in tenant name renders in admin dashboard — any admin viewing tenants is vulnerable
Security response bridge — CTO, security lead, and SSO team joining at 18:06
test: reproduce XSS via tenant name field — validate scope of vulnerability
XSS confirmed: injecting script tag via tenant name executes in admin dashboard — high severity, auth required to exploit
VTX-702 — XSS scope confirmed: exploitable by any authenticated tenant admin, affects admin dashboard
Security lead: fix is HTML encoding on tenant name output — all render points need sanitisation
fix: HTML encode tenant name in all 4 render locations — dashboard list, tenant settings, audit log, email templates
Queue drain: 1,600 of 3,847 delivered — 41% complete, no delivery failures in drain
NOVA-1003 update — 1,600 of 3,847 delivered
Retainer contract signed at 18:08 — client VP and PM both signed, $8,200/month Standard tier confirmed
Subject: Retainer contract signed — confirmed | Body: Both parties signed, $8,200/month Standard tier, commencing Monday
ATL-1001 retainer signed — $8,200/month Standard tier commencing Monday, delivery invoice $180K also sent
PM: delivery invoice $180,000 and first retainer invoice $8,200 both sent — $188,200 in receivables today
XSS fix implemented in all 4 render locations — deploying to staging for security validation
test: XSS fix validation — attempt injection in all 4 render locations, confirm encoding blocks execution
VTX-701 fix in staging — XSS validation testing in progress
Security test: all 4 render locations encoding correctly — script tags rendered as text, no execution
VTX-701 fix validated — XSS blocked in all 4 locations, deploying to production
merge: XSS fix — HTML encoding on tenant name in dashboard, settings, audit log, email templates
Queue drain: 2,400 of 3,847 delivered — 62% complete
NOVA-1003 update — 2,400 of 3,847 delivered
XSS fix deployed to production — vulnerability patched, admin dashboard no longer vulnerable
VTX-701 closed — XSS vulnerability patched in production at 18:12, 7-minute fix from disclosure to production
Security lead: notifying researcher Ananya Iyer — patch deployed, CVE detail being prepared, acknowledging responsible disclosure
Subject: XSS vulnerability patched — thank you | Body: Fix deployed to production at 18:12, CVE detail forthcoming, acknowledging responsible disclosure
Ananya confirmed receipt — satisfied with 7-minute patch turnaround, willing to delay public disclosure for 7 days for CVE assignment
VTX-703 — CVE assignment requested, 7-day disclosure hold agreed with researcher
Queue drain: 3,200 of 3,847 delivered — 83% complete
NOVA-1003 update — 3,200 of 3,847 delivered
Yusuf (Holdar Corp): watching events arrive in their system — confirms events in correct order, integration processing normally
fix: webhook delivery worker lock timeout — acquire lock with 5-second timeout, log and retry on timeout
NOVA-1004 — webhook lock timeout fix implementing, prevents future deadlock
Lock timeout fix deployed — any lock acquisition now expires after 5 seconds, worker retries instead of deadlocking
NOVA-1004 closed — lock timeout deployed, deadlock scenario impossible going forward
Queue drain: 3,847 of 3,847 delivered — complete at 18:17, all events delivered in correct order
NOVA-1003 closed — 3,847 events delivered, queue empty
Yusuf (Holdar Corp): all events received and processed — integration back to normal, no data loss
Subject: Holdar Corp webhook delivery complete | Body: All 3,847 events delivered, 6-hour gap resolved, root cause: deadlock in delivery worker, fix deployed
NOVA-1001 resolved — all 3,847 events delivered, Holdar Corp integration operational
Security review of all input fields triggered — checking for unescaped output across SSO module
test: full SSO module XSS audit — all 14 input fields checked for HTML encoding on output
VTX-704 — SSO module full XSS audit, 14 input fields
XSS audit result: 2 additional unescaped fields found — SAML entity ID and IdP display name
fix: HTML encode SAML entity ID and IdP display name in all render locations
VTX-705 — 2 additional fields patched: SAML entity ID, IdP display name
2 additional patches deployed to production — proactive fixes before any researcher finds them
VTX-705 closed — SAML entity ID and IdP display name both encoded
Client VP forwarding retainer contract to accounts payable — first invoice to be paid within net-30
ATL-1002 — retainer invoice in client accounts payable queue, net-30 payment expected
Webhook delivery SLA review — 6-hour silent failure with no alerting flagged as monitoring gap
NOVA-1005 opened — webhook queue depth monitoring gap, no alert configured for growing queue
feat: webhook queue depth alert — fires PagerDuty when any customer queue exceeds 100 undelivered events for 5 minutes
Queue depth alert configured — any customer queue over 100 events for 5 minutes triggers immediate PagerDuty
NOVA-1005 closed — queue depth monitoring active, 100-event threshold
Notification grouping deployed to production after 48-hour soak — 99.9% accuracy across 40,000 soak alerts
ORN-701 — notification grouping in production, 99.9% accuracy from 48-hour soak
merge: notification grouping — production deploy after 48-hour soak validation
Root cause document sent to Holdar Corp — deadlock explanation, 6-hour gap evidence, 2 fixes shipped: lock timeout + queue alert
Subject: Root cause — Holdar Corp webhook gap | Body: Deadlock at 12:04, 3,847 events queued, lock timeout fix and queue depth alert both shipped
NOVA-1006 — root cause report sent to Holdar Corp
Full SSO module security audit complete — 14 fields audited, 3 total patches shipped (tenant name + 2 additional)
VTX-704 closed — all 14 SSO fields audited, 3 patches shipped
docs: SSO security audit report — 14 fields checked, 3 patched, 11 confirmed clean
Security lead: extending XSS audit to entire application — 89 total input fields across all modules
VTX-706 — full application XSS audit initiated, 89 input fields across all modules
test: full application XSS audit — automated scanner across all 89 input fields
Automated XSS scanner running — estimated 25 minutes for all 89 fields
Notification grouping first production metrics — 1,240 alerts grouped into 380 notifications, 69% notification reduction
ORN-702 — first production hour: 69% notification reduction from grouping
Retainer kickoff agenda drafted — Monday 10:00 onboarding call, SLA walkthrough, escalation paths
Retainer kickoff call — Monday 10:00, PM, engineering lead, client team
ATL-1003 retainer kickoff scheduled — Monday 10:00
Yusuf (Holdar Corp) acknowledged root cause — appreciated transparency, no SLA credit requested given rapid resolution
Subject: Holdar Corp root cause acknowledged | Body: Yusuf satisfied, no SLA credit requested, appreciates proactive monitoring fix
NOVA-1007 — Holdar Corp relationship stable, no SLA credit requested
XSS scanner: 45 of 89 fields scanned — no new vulnerabilities found so far
VTX-706 update — 45 of 89 fields scanned, no new vulnerabilities
Webhook delivery monitoring dashboard added — real-time queue depth per customer, delivery rate, failure count
feat: webhook monitoring dashboard — per-customer queue depth, delivery rate, and failure count visible to on-call
NOVA-1008 — webhook monitoring dashboard shipped, on-call now has real-time visibility
Sprint 18 day 3 — notification grouping shipped, anomaly tuning complete, 6 of 14 tasks done
ORN-703 sprint day 3 — 6 of 14 tasks complete, velocity 116%
XSS scanner: 89 of 89 fields scanned — 3 total patches were all that were needed, no additional vulnerabilities
VTX-706 closed — full application XSS audit complete, 89 fields, 3 vulnerabilities all patched
docs: full application XSS audit complete — 89 fields scanned, 3 patched, application clean
Security lead: sending Ananya comprehensive fix note — tenant name, entity ID, and IdP display name all patched plus full audit
Subject: Comprehensive XSS remediation — Ananya | Body: All 3 related fields patched, full 89-field audit complete, application XSS-free
Ananya confirmed impressed — will recommend Vertex security practices in responsible disclosure writeup
VTX-707 — positive researcher feedback, responsible disclosure writeup will highlight Vertex response speed
Engineering audit of all worker lock patterns — 6 workers reviewed for missing timeouts
test: audit all 6 delivery workers for lock acquisition without timeout
Worker lock audit: 2 additional workers missing timeout — notification-worker.py and export-delivery-worker.py
NOVA-1009 — 2 additional workers missing lock timeout, proactive fix
fix: add 5-second lock timeout to notification-worker.py and export-delivery-worker.py
Both workers patched — all 6 delivery workers now have lock timeout, deadlock scenario eliminated system-wide
NOVA-1009 closed — all 6 workers have lock timeout, deadlock eliminated system-wide
Delivery invoice payment timeline confirmed — client accounts payable confirmed net-30, payment expected May 2
Subject: Invoice payment timeline confirmed | Body: $180,000 delivery invoice payment by May 2, retainer auto-pay from Monday
ATL-1004 — payment timeline confirmed, $180K by May 2, retainer auto-pay active
Notification grouping 2-hour production metrics — 2,840 alerts grouped into 810 notifications, 71% notification reduction
ORN-704 — 2 hours production: 71% notification reduction, improving as model warms up
CTO: today's webhook incident turned into system-wide deadlock prevention — all 6 workers hardened
docs: worker lock audit — all 6 workers reviewed and hardened with timeout
NOVA-1010 engineering hardening — 6 workers all timeout-protected, audit complete
CVE assigned — CVE-2026-4471, CVSS score 6.1 Medium, public disclosure in 7 days
VTX-708 CVE-2026-4471 assigned — CVSS 6.1, 7-day disclosure window, patch already in production
Security lead: CVE-2026-4471 patch deployed before disclosure — textbook responsible disclosure process
docs: CVE-2026-4471 — stored XSS in SSO tenant name, patched 18:12, full audit complete
PM end-of-week summary: $180K delivery invoice, $8,200/month retainer, Monday kickoff confirmed

Notification grouping performance confirmed — 71% reduction sustainable, customer noise complaints expected to drop
ORN-705 — notification grouping confirmed 71% reduction in production, milestone complete
Webhook delivery system post-hardening status — 6 workers timeout-protected, queue monitoring active, dashboard live
docs: webhook delivery hardening summary — deadlock fix, lock timeouts, queue alert, monitoring dashboard
NOVA-1011 webhook system hardening complete — 4 improvements shipped from single incident
Sprint 18 day 3 end — 7 of 14 tasks complete, XSS audit complete, rate limit monitoring active, velocity 116%

docs: on-call runbook updated — webhook queue monitoring, lock timeout behaviour, Holdar Corp incident summary
Ananya's responsible disclosure writeup preview shared — highlights 7-minute patch time as industry benchmark

Subject: Retainer scope details — Standard tier | Body: 40 hours/month included, additional at $195/hour, rollover up to 10 hours

End-of-day webhook incident summary — 3,847 events delivered, deadlock resolved, 6 workers hardened, 2 monitoring gaps closed
